title: 7 Herbal Creams For Numbness In Hands
intro_desc: Herbal creams can be a great help for numbness in hands. These creams often contain herbs that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and ease numbness. For example, Lavandula angustifolia, also known as lavender, has been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects and can help soothe and calm the skin. Another herb, Achillea millefolium, or yarrow, has antispasmodic properties, which can help relax muscles and reduce numbness.  Hypericum perforatum, or St John's Wort, is a natural pain reliever that can also help reduce numbness. These herbs work by reducing inflammation and relaxing muscles, which can help improve blood flow and ease numbness. Using herbal creams can be a natural and effective way to treat numbness in hands, without the side effects of prescription medications.  Applying these creams to the affected area can provide relief from numbness and tingling, and can also help promote healing and reduce scarring. The anti-inflammatory properties of these herbs can also help reduce redness and swelling, making them a great option for treating numbness in hands.  Herbal creams can be especially helpful for people who experience numbness in hands due to repetitive strain injuries, or those who have had injuries to the hands or wrists. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, these creams can help reduce numbness and improve overall hand function.
intro_image_alt: herbal creams for numbness in hands

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams contains a high concentration of linalool and linalyl acetate, two key active constituents responsible for its therapeutic properties. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and vasodilating properties, which help to improve blood circulation to the hands. The increased blood flow reduces numbness and tingling sensations caused by poor circulation. The calming effect of Lavandula angustifolia creams also helps to reduce muscle tension and promote relaxation, further alleviating numbness in the hands. By applying Lavandula angustifolia creams to the affected area, users can experience relief from numbness and improve their overall hand comfort.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal creams for numbness in hands
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 10 drops of Lavandula angustifolia essential oil.
Melt coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl in the microwave for 30-second intervals, stirring between each interval, until smooth.
Add beeswax to the melted mixture and stir until fully incorporated.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly. Add Lavandula angustifolia essential oil and stir well.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let cool completely before use. Apply a small amount to affected hands as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium creams contains essential oils with sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and triterpenoids as its active constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and discomfort in the hands. The sesquiterpenes in Achillea millefolium, specifically chamazulene, have anti-spasmodic properties that can help relax muscles and improve blood flow to the affected area. The flavonoids present in the cream, such as kaempferol and quercetin, have antioxidant properties that can help protect the nerves and reduce numbness. By reducing inflammation and promoting healthy nerve function, Achillea millefolium creams may help alleviate numbness in the hands.
remedies.image_alt: Achillea millefolium herbal creams for numbness in hands
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of dried Achillea millefolium flowers, 1 cup of coconut oil, and 1/4 cup of beeswax.
Heat the coconut oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the dried flowers to the melted coconut oil and let it steep for 2-3 hours, then strain the mixture.
In a separate bowl, mix 1/4 cup of beeswax with the infused oil and heat the mixture until the beeswax is melted.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify. Store the creams in a cool, dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum creams contains active constituents like hyperforin and emodin, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help reduce swelling and ease numbness in the hands by improving blood flow and reducing pressure on the nerves. Hypericum perforatum creams also contain flavonoids like quercetin, which have antioxidant properties that protect the nerves from damage. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of these constituents help alleviate numbness in the hands by promoting healthy nerve function. Regular use of Hypericum perforatum creams may help restore sensation and mobility in the affected area.
remedies.image_alt: Hypericum perforatum herbal creams for numbness in hands
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of Hypericum perforatum (St. John's Wort) oil, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval, until smooth.
Remove from heat and stir in the Hypericum perforatum oil and vitamin E oil.
Pour the mixture into a silicone candy mold or a small tin container.
Allow the mixture to cool and harden completely, then remove from mold and store in an airtight container in the refrigerator.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams contains flavonoids like quercetin and kaempferol, as well as terpenoids like bilobalide and ginkgolides. These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help protect the nerves and improve blood flow to the hands. The flavonoids in Ginkgo biloba creams have a vasodilating effect, which means they help widen the blood vessels and increase blood flow to the hands. This increased blood flow can help alleviate numbness in the hands by bringing more oxygen and nutrients to the nerves. By protecting the nerves and improving blood flow, Ginkgo biloba creams can help reduce numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal creams for numbness in hands
remedy_procedure: Gather 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of Ginkgo biloba extract in a small saucepan.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in the saucepan over low heat, stirring until the beeswax is fully dissolved.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and stir in the Ginkgo biloba extract.
Let the mixture cool until it reaches a temperature of around 120°F to 130°F. This is an important step to prevent skin irritation.
Pour the mixture into a small container and let it cool completely before applying it to the affected area to help relieve numbness in hands.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains a compound called gingerol, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties help reduce swelling and pain in the hands. Gingerol also contains a compound called 6-gingerol, which helps improve blood flow and reduce numbness. The warming sensation caused by gingerol can increase blood circulation, which helps numbness caused by poor circulation. The combination of these properties makes Zingiber officinale creams a potential treatment for hand numbness.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for numbness in hands
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 1/2 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of Zingiber officinale powder, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl over a pot of simmering water.
Add the shea butter and stir until it's fully incorporated and the mixture is smooth.
Add the Zingiber officinale powder and vitamin E oil to the mixture, and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify. Once set, use it as needed to relieve numbness in the hands.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams contains tannins and volatile compounds like eucalyptol, alpha-pinene, and limonene. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can help reduce pain and swelling in the hands. Tannins also have astringent properties, which can constrict blood vessels and improve circulation, helping to reduce numbness and tingling. Eucalyptol, a key component of eucalyptus oil, has been shown to have a neuroprotective effect, which can help restore sensation and reduce numbness in the hands. By reducing inflammation and improving circulation, eucalyptus globulus creams can help alleviate numbness and other symptoms associated with poor blood flow to the hands.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal creams for numbness in hands
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 1/4 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of Eucalyptus globulus essential oil, and 1 tablespoon of coconut oil.
Melt the beeswax and shea butter in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval, until smooth.
Add the coconut oil and Eucalyptus globulus essential oil to the melted mixture. Stir well to combine.
Slowly pour the distilled water into the mixture while continuously whisking until well combined and a smooth consistency is achieved.
Pour the mixture into a container, let it cool and harden. Once hardened, your Eucalyptus globulus cream is ready to use for numbness in hands.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams contains compounds like alkylamides, caffeic acid, and rosmarinic acid. These bioactive constituents have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling in the hands and fingers. The compounds also have antioxidant properties, which can protect nerve cells from damage and promote healing. Additionally, the alkylamides in Echinacea purpurea creams have been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate numbness and discomfort in the hands. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Echinacea purpurea creams may help restore sensation and function in the hands and fingers.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for numbness in hands
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Infuse the dried flowers in 2 cups of coconut oil in a double boiler for 2 hours, stirring occasionally.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl, discarding the solids.
In a separate bowl, mix 1/4 cup of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil. Melt the beeswax in a double boiler.
Combine the infused oil and melted beeswax mixture. Pour into a container and let cool. Use on numb hands as needed.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent numbness in hands from forming. Some herbal teas, like ginkgo biloba and ginger, improve blood flow to the hands. This increased circulation can help prevent numbness and tingling. Drinking these teas regularly might also reduce the risk of numbness. It's thought that their antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties contribute to this effect.
faq_safe: Drinking herbal teas for numbness in hands daily can be safe, but it's essential to check the ingredients. Some teas, like ginger or peppermint, are generally considered harmless. However, others may contain stimulants or interact with medications. Choose teas from reputable sources and follow the recommended amounts to minimize potential risks.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can start to show results for numbness in hands within a few weeks to a few months. The exact timeframe depends on the individual and the specific herbal tea being used. Some people may notice a difference in a few weeks, while others may take longer.
faq_time: If you're experiencing numbness in your hands, consider drinking herbal tea in the morning or early afternoon. This is when blood flow is at its best, helping your body to circulate nutrients and oxygen more efficiently. Drinking herbal tea during these times may help alleviate numbness symptoms.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications, including those for numbness in hands. Certain herbs like ginkgo biloba, garlic, and ginger can increase the risk of bleeding, which is a concern if you're taking medications that affect blood clotting. This can lead to problems like bruising or bleeding in hands.
faq_pregnant: When pregnant, it's best to be cautious with herbal teas, especially if you're looking for relief from numbness in your hands. Some herbal teas, like ginseng and licorice root, can stimulate the uterus and cause problems. Stick to caffeine-free teas like peppermint, chamomile, or raspberry leaf tea, which are generally considered safe in moderation.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas can help with numbness in hands, but as a breastfeeding mom, it's essential to check the ingredients. Some herbs, like sage and peppermint, can pass into breast milk, while others like ginger and chamomile are generally considered safe. Always research the specific tea you're considering and look for reliable information about its effects on breastfeeding and baby.
